# dotr v0.1.0

This is the first public release of `dotr`, a Rust-native backup tool for personal configuration files.

## Highlights

- Back up selected files and directories into a Git repository without taking over your dotfile layout.
- Keep a simple repository structure under `files/home`, lazy `files/root`, and `metadata/index.json`.
- Initialize a reusable backup repo with `dotr init --with-defaults --set-default`.
- Add and remove managed paths with scoped backup/delete behavior via `dotr add` and `dotr remove`.
- Run safe backup and restore previews with `--dry-run`, restore diffs with `--diff`, and alternate restore output with `-o`.
- Encrypt selected files with built-in age support and generate key material with `dotr keygen`.
- Watch configured paths with daemon commands and structured local logs.
- Export generated inventories such as Homebrew packages and VS Code extensions through `custom_backup`.
- Automate Git history with optional commit and push support.

## Safety Notes

- `identity` is private key material. Keep it outside the backup Git repo.
- `recipients` contains public age recipients and can be committed.
- If a path was backed up in plaintext before being encrypted, old Git history may still contain plaintext.
- Restoring absolute paths under `files/root` is intentionally explicit and requires `--allow-absolute` with `--apply`.

## Install

```sh
cargo install dotr-cli
```

The crate is named `dotr-cli`; the installed command is `dotr`.
